redacted: The live 'Account:' row carried the recording operator's ChatGPT login. It is replaced byte-for-byte with 'user@example.com' (16 chars in, 16 chars out), so every column of the box renders exactly as captured. This is the ONLY edit to bytes.raw.
notes: Hand-recorded probe pinning the 'plan' rung on the PERMISSIONS AXIS ONLY. Same launch flags as status-plan but WITHOUT the post-launch /plan, so this box reads observed 'plan' with collaboration 'Default'. It is the fixture that proves the two axes do not collapse. It also CORRECTS the predicted table row: 0.144.5 renders 'Read Only (untrusted)', NOT 'Custom (read-only, untrusted)' — the read-only sandbox gets its own presentation family rather than the Custom (<sandbox>, <policy>) form. Widths are measured from the rendered 120x40 screen. widest_status_row_cols/status_box_outer_cols is the box's rendered width; intrinsic_row_cols is the minimum unwrapped width each row needs (opening bar + label column + value + closing bar), which is what CODEX_STATUS_MIN_COLS has to clear. NOTE the Session: row needs 62 cols, above the current CODEX_STATUS_MIN_COLS = 60 — a documented FOLLOW-UP to raise the constant with this evidence, not a defect here: both the session scrape and the permission parse anchor the closing bar on the same physical line, so a wrapped row fails closed at any width.
